title: where can I watch rebuild without downloading it
display_title: where can I watch rebuild without downloading it
wikitext:
[]: where can I watch rebuild without downloading it?             Quote Post by Duke » Fri Aug 15, 2008 5:15 pm well, I can't find it anywhere, I was wondering if somebody could tell me where I can watch it without downloading stuff? thanks, Duke.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Aug 15, 2008 5:17 pm Hmmm, probably at some Japanese dudes house. Other than that your going to have to download it. That is what I did.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Aug 15, 2008 5:31 pm Buying the Japanese language DVD from e.g. amazon.co.jp

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Aug 15, 2008 5:44 pm That too.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Fri Aug 15, 2008 9:54 pm if you really dont wanna download/buy it watch it on veoh.com

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Aug 15, 2008 10:00 pm Crunchyroll

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Sat Aug 16, 2008 1:14 am I looked on veoh, I found it :). I downloaded VeohTV to watch it and I'm watching it right now :).

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Sat Aug 16, 2008 6:24 am Duke wrote: I looked on veoh, I found it :). I downloaded VeohTV to watch it and I'm watching it right now :).

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Sat Aug 16, 2008 8:43 am Technically, watching it on Veoh also counts as downloading it...

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Sat Aug 16, 2008 11:07 am not really, downloading it would be like... downloading it off the net, it just streams on veoh. It's a sin to watch any Eva movie while doing other stuff in the Internet. It breaks the first commandment of Lilith, our God! 1. Thou shalt devoteth all thy attention to Eva, and only Eva, while watching Eva!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Aug 16, 2008 3:51 pm Duke wrote: not really, downloading it would be like... downloading it off the net, it just streams on veoh.

[]:             Quote Post by iamsolidsnake » Sun Aug 17, 2008 12:35 am Checking the DVD thread would help, as I posted a stream link not two days before this thread's creation. :: For those of us with limited hard drive space/short on patience, I found a stream. BUT, first you'll need to get Veoh tv ( small download) so you can watch more than a 5 minute preview. Its dvd quality, but its not like its the best clarity ever, I'm sure its an avi at the source. http://onlinecliptv.com/list/video/12545/Rebuild-of-Evangelion Don't forget to click the accept button, its just acknowledging you're leaving the site. If this has been posted before, sorry, feel free to delete my post. That being said, Someone Got Scryed in my evas! Oh Noes!!! Can we get more rainbow in there?

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sun Aug 17, 2008 4:46 am If you happen to be in Germany, it's currently being shown as part of the "Fantasy Film Fest" in various cities with in Japanese with english subs: http://www.fantasyfilmfest.com/fantasy/filme2008/evan.html

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Aug 17, 2008 5:03 am Gah, you heathens! Thou shalt BUY it to help the economy of Japan! Watching it free is a SIN!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 17, 2008 10:25 am Now Asuka can watch the first film to get up to speed with her arrival in the second one! Imp: Actually I'm going to buy all 4 films when they're released as a boxset. Knowing Gainax it most likely will be released that way at some point down the line...

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sun Aug 17, 2008 10:50 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Now Asuka can watch the first film to get up to speed with her arrival in the second one!

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Aug 17, 2008 12:14 pm It's also making the rounds at various anime conventions, without much fanfare. I saw it at Anime Expo last month.

[]:             Quote Post by iamsolidsnake » Sun Aug 17, 2008 8:59 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Now Asuka can watch the first film to get up to speed with her arrival in the second one! Imp: Actually I'm going to buy all 4 films when they're released as a boxset. Knowing Gainax it most likely will be released that way at some point down the line...

[]:             Quote Post by System Shocker » Sun Aug 17, 2008 9:55 pm In San Francisco at the Japantown Mall they have Rebuild of Evangelion dvds which have Japanese audio and English subtitles. When I saw it I was like snatches! and I bought it for 29.99 and then I looked at the back and could tell that the english subtitles were translated from chinese subtitles and it was a region 0 dvd. Then I returned it and got all my money back. The dvds they sold from that fake ass store only cost 1.50 to make and are only copies.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Aug 18, 2008 9:05 am @SSD I will buy individually first, then, I will also buy the box set. It is like having many different versions of the same music. My piano teacher gave me this habit, but aside from the fact that your wallet dries up very quickly, it is good for referencing etc. BUT I STILL CAN'T BELIEVE PEOPLE ARE TRYING TO WATCH IT FOR FREE! HEATHENS! BLASPHEMERS etc. etc.
summary:
[]: where can I watch rebuild without downloading it?             Quote Post by Duke » Fri Aug 15, 2008 5:15 pm well, I can't find it anywhere, I was wondering if somebody could tell me where I can watch it without downloading stuff? thanks, Duke.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Aug 15, 2008 5:17 pm Hmmm, probably at some Japanese dudes house. Other than that yo
